name: El Celler de Can Roca
recognition: 3 Michelin stars; World's 50 Best No.1 in 2013 & 2015 (then retired to Best of the Best)
what_to_order: The full tasting: caramelised olives plucked from a bonsai, closing on Jordi's perfume-built desserts.
reservation: Books ~11 months ahead, midnight on the 1st of the month; card to hold
best_time: Lunch — marginally easier to land than dinner
notes: Closed Sun & Mon; the booking window, not the walk, is the gatekeeper
verdict: Forty years in, all three brothers are still on the floor — this is not a three-star coasting on its two world titles.
caveat: Bookings open at midnight on the 1st of each month, eleven months out, card to hold; shut Sunday and Monday, and 16 August–1 September this summer.

name: Restaurant Massana
recognition: 1 Michelin star, first awarded 2007, held through the 2026 Guide; fourth-generation kitchen
what_to_order: Homenaje al Magret de Pato — duck breast off the grill, on the card since 1986.
notes: Quieter than Can Roca but still needs advance booking
verdict: Girona's longest-held star after Can Roca's — kept since 2007, and bookable in days, not seasons.
caveat: Shut Sunday and Monday, last tasting-menu seating at nine sharp — book days ahead, not on the night.

name: Divinum
recognition: 1 Michelin star, held in the 2026 Guide; kitchen trained under Joan Roca
what_to_order: The classic steak tartare, mustard-cool, with whatever Laura pours against it.
notes: Below street level in the Casino building — easy to walk past
verdict: The only star in Girona won from the dining room, not the pass — Joan and Laura's floor, since the wine-bar days.
caveat: Shut Sunday and Monday, kitchen closes at ten — and the vault sits below street level, easy to march straight past.

name: Normal
recognition: The Roca brothers' deliberately informal room; interiors by Andreu Carulla
what_to_order: The whisky tart with egg-yolk ice cream — the one dessert the inspectors single out.
notes: The companion Bar Normal opens at noon for vermouth and all-day picking
verdict: The Rocas at their most Catalan — their mother's home cooking by El Celler-trained hands, not a three-star spin-off doing tricks.
caveat: Shut Mondays; the diary opens 90 days ahead at midnight, card down — €50 a head if you cancel inside three days.

name: Casa Cacao
recognition: Jordi Roca + Anna Payet's bean-to-bar house and 15-room hotel over the Onyar
what_to_order: The thick hot chocolate — single-origin, conched metres away, dense enough to coat the spoon.
notes: Shop is walk-in; the 15-room hotel above books out fast
verdict: Not a three-star gift shop — Jordi Roca roasts, grinds and conches the beans on the premises, and the cup proves it.
caveat: A café, not a lunch room — the bookable brunch is the hotel roof's midday sitting (12:00–14:00, about €35, reserve ahead); the shop is walk-in, the fifteen rooms above are not.

name: Rocambolesc
recognition: Jordi Roca's gelateria — the Celler's dessert language at street level
what_to_order: The Panet — brioche pressed hot around cold soft-serve; crackling crust, frozen heart.
notes: Queues form fast in summer; a candy confiteria shares the address
verdict: Three-Michelin-star pastry thinking at four euros a cone — and the queue down Santa Clara knows it.
caveat: Walk-in only, and shut by 21:00 five nights a week (23:00 Friday–Saturday) — no midweek after-dinner cone.

name: Can Marquès
recognition: One of Girona's longest-running family tables, opened 1920, now fourth generation
what_to_order: The Thursday rice — the pan carries whatever the market gave up that morning.
reservation: Advisable for the three dinner nights
notes: Night service Wed–Fri only; it shops the market next door
verdict: Four unbroken generations since 1920 — among Girona's longest-running tables, and the Mercat del Lleó next door still sets the menu.
caveat: Lunch is the meal: dinner is Thursday and Friday only, groups only — and Saturdays shut mid-June to mid-September.

name: Casa Marieta
neighborhood: Barri Vell · Plaça de la Independència
recognition: On the arcaded square since 1892, five generations in
what_to_order: Duck with pears: the old Catalan way, pears gone amber in the roasting juices.
notes: Also does takeaway if the room is full
verdict: Girona's oldest restaurant — on a square ringed with look-alike terraces, the one that has cooked here since 1892 and still cooks like it.
caveat: Open daily but on rails — lunch 12.30 to 3.30, dinner 7.30 to 10.30, half an hour longer at weekends, nothing between; the arcaded terrace books out first, and nobody here is chasing tasting-menu invention.

name: Fonda Cal Ros
neighborhood: Barri Vell · Carrer de la Cort Reial
address: Carrer de la Cort Reial 9, 17004 Girona
product_tags: Stone vaults; Els Tinars team; Seasonal
recognition: Historic inn relaunched by the Els Tinars / Marc Gascons team
what_to_order: Rockfish rice a la llauna — baked flat in its tin until the edges catch.
notes: Closed Tue; on the same street where the xuixo was invented
verdict: Not the Gascons' starred table — the same Els Tinars hands cooking fricandó and rice a la llauna for fonda money under the old inn's vaults.
caveat: Shut Tuesdays and Wednesdays — not the Monday you'd guess; plates land to share, so order as a table, not as individuals.

name: Nexe
recognition: The old Nu, transferred to its own chefs in Aug 2022; MICHELIN Guide selection + Guía Repsol Recomendado 2026
what_to_order: Steak tartare, worked up in front of you at the open bar.
notes: Nu's old domain is dead; don't bank on the tasting menu third-party sites advertise
verdict: Nu is gone — Massana signed the room over to his own cooks in 2022, and as Nexe they've kept their MICHELIN Guide listing without him.
caveat: Shut Sunday and Monday, two-hour service windows — book, and don't bank on the tasting menu TheFork advertises; the carte runs short.

name: Cal Formatger
neighborhood: Barri Vell · Carrer de la Cort Reial
address: Carrer de la Cort Reial 12, 17001 Girona
recognition: A cheese room built on Girona-province makers — Garrotxa goat's cheese front and centre
what_to_order: The Girona-comarca board, cut to order — start with the grey-rinded, hazelnut-sweet goat's cheeses.
reservation: Walk-in to shop; ask ahead for a private tasting
notes: Small room, opens 10:30; closed Sun & Mon
verdict: The quesería Guía Repsol bills as Joan Roca's favourite in his own city — proof a province of small dairies can carry a counter alone.
caveat: Shut Sunday and Monday, and the counter goes dark mid-afternoon most days; tastings want notice, not a walk-in.

name: Arròs 9 by Terram
recognition: The casual rice-focused offshoot of the gastronomic Terram, same chef
what_to_order: Arròs del senyoret amb llagosta — lobster rice, everything peeled, spoon straight to the crust.
notes: Rice dishes usually need two — call ahead if solo
verdict: Not a photo-menu paella stop — Terram's chef directs the rice pans, and each arròs is built on its own sofregit.
caveat: Shut Wednesdays, and dinner only four nights a week — Sundays and Tuesdays it's lunch or nothing.

name: Can Castelló
recognition: Brands itself the keeper of the early xuixo recipe (its 116-year Santa Clara flagship closed 2014)
what_to_order: The classic crema xuixo, eaten warm — sugar crust cracking into soft crema catalana.
notes: Argenteria 7 is the only Girona address — the Calderers counter is gone; the 'best xuixo' title is contested (Tornés won the 2026 world contest)
verdict: Not the 2026 world-champion xuixo — Tornés took that — but the only house on earth frying nothing else.
caveat: A branded flagship, not the vanished old bakery — the frying happens in Banyoles, the Calderers counter is gone, and Argenteria 7 is the only address.

name: La Fàbrica
neighborhood: Barri Vell · Carrer de la Llebre
recognition: Girona's first third-wave café, founded by a pro cyclist and his wife — hub of the city's riding scene
what_to_order: The brunch board with a filter coffee — beans they roast themselves at Espresso Mafia.
reservation: None — expect a wait at peak brunch
notes: Open daily 9:00–17:00, incl. Sundays — useful when the rest is shut
verdict: Girona's first third-wave café — the 2015 original the city's whole cycling-coffee scene grew out of.
caveat: No bookings, a wall of Lycra at weekend brunch, and doors shut at five — dinner is elsewhere.

name: Mercat del Lleó
recognition: The working municipal market, in a purpose-built 1944 hall of ~1,712 m² and ~60 stalls
what_to_order: Day-boat Costa Brava fish off the Salvador counter — bright-eyed, stiff, smelling of the sea.
best_time: 07:00–10:00 for the best fish and produce
notes: Mon–Fri 07:00–14:00, Sat 07:00–14:30; closed Sun. A locals' market, not a tourist stop
verdict: The 1944 hall Girona still cooks from — a working larder, not a food-hall conversion.
caveat: Shut Sundays and wound down by two; after ten the fish is picked over — come to shop, not to graze.

name: Espelt Viticultors
recognition: One of the largest DO Empordà producers, farming organically on the Alt Empordà
what_to_order: Airam: grandfather Lluís's solera Garnatxa — gold shading to red, every vintage since 1997.
notes: Province, not city — an hour out; the town coordinate is approximate
verdict: The big estate that farms like a small one — 200 organic hectares of DO Empordà with a solera in the cellar running back to 1997.
caveat: Book and pay online first — the 11:00 vineyard tours run on set dates only (weekly in August, thin the rest of the year), €27–38 a head, and it's a good fifty minutes from Girona; make it a Cap de Creus morning, not a detour.

name: Esperit Roca
neighborhood: La Fortalesa · Sant Julià de Ramis (10 km N)
address: Castell de Sant Julià de Ramis, Muntanya dels Sants Metges, Carrer Major s/n, 17481 Sant Julià de Ramis, Girona
recognition: 1 MICHELIN star, first awarded Guide España 2025, retained 2026; the Roca brothers' fort project (opened May 2024)
what_to_order: Toda la gamba — Palamós red prawn marinated in seaweed vinegar and katsuobushi; prawn velouté, sauce of the head, crisp legs, citric caviar, a concentrated distillate of the coral.
price_range: €€€€ — Salty Spirit menu €170, Sweet Spirit menu €139, à la carte available
reservation: Books 90 days out; EUR 50 pp late-cancellation fee
best_time: Lunch (Fri-Mon), for the views over Girona
notes: Closed Tue-Wed; dinner Thu-Sat only; holiday closure 3-11 Nov 2026
verdict: This is El Celler's back catalogue served under its own Michelin star — bookable ninety days out at €170, where the mothership takes eleven months and some €145 more.
caveat: Not El Celler and not pretending to be — this is the archive, refined, not the frontier. You need a car or taxi up the hill from Girona; Tuesdays and Wednesdays are dark; dinner runs only Thursday to Saturday; and €139–170 before wine is real money for revival dishes.
